regulatory_context: U.S. federal money transmission and state licensing framework; subject to proposed U.S. stablecoin legislation (e.g., CLARITY Act). Circle is a U.S. entity regulated at both state and federal level.
policy_context_source: U.S. Congressional stablecoin bills; Federal Reserve policy signals; OCC / FinCEN guidance
notes: Primary market is USD-denominated; on-chain activity may reflect U.S.-centered institutional demand and compliance cycles. No causal claim.

regulatory_context: MiCA (Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation) — regulated as an Electronic Money Token (EMT) since MiCA entry into force. Circle obtained EMI license in France (Autorité de contrôle prudentiel et de résolution) for EURC issuance.
policy_context_source: EU MiCA Regulation (Regulation (EU) 2023/1114); ECB monetary policy
notes: Euro-denominated; MiCA reserve and redemption requirements differ from U.S. framework. EURC issued under EU regulatory oversight. No causal claim.

regulatory_context: Regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) under the Payment Services Act (PS Act). StraitsX holds a Major Payment Institution (MPI) licence. XSGD is a digital payment token pegged 1:1 to Singapore Dollar with reserves held at licensed Singapore financial institutions.
policy_context_source: MAS Payment Services Act (PS Act 2019, amended 2021); MAS stablecoin regulatory framework (August 2023); MAS digital payment token licensing
notes: SGD-pegged regional stablecoin; smaller on-chain surface than USDC by multiple orders of magnitude. No causal claim. Activity reflects Singapore payment ecosystem context.
